title: An EEG dataset recorded during affective music listening
description: This dataset comprises unprocessed EEG recordings from 31 healthy adult participants (age 18-66, 18 female) listening to 40 music clips of varying emotional content. The study investigates neural correlates of music-induced emotion through six runs of EEG recordings, including resting-state baseline and four music listening runs. Data were collected at 1 kHz sampling rate with 20 s recording duration per trial (12 s music stimulus plus 8 s additional recording window) and are accompanied by participants' self-reported emotional responses, providing a resource for studying the relationship between brain activity and affective music perception.
methods_description: EEG data were recorded from 31 participants during six runs: two resting-state runs (300 s each) and four music listening runs (10 trials each with 12 s music clips). Music stimuli comprised film score excerpts rated for induced emotion. EEG was sampled at 1 kHz with 20 s recording duration per trial (12 s stimulus plus 8 s buffer). Participants reported induced emotional responses following each trial. Contributors to data collection and processing: Isil Poyraz Bilgin, James Weaver, Asad Malik.
